questions.text: When you're learning Spanish, what specific scenarios feel most uncomfortable or where do you still get stuck? Is it more around formal vs. casual speech, understanding fast conversations, or expressing nuanced emotions/ideas?

When you're buying something — say a new gadget, clothes, or even food — how often do you actively choose products based on their environmental impact or company ethics? Is it a dealbreaker, a nice-to-have, or something you think about but don't let it override other factors like price or convenience?
What's your current fitness philosophy — are you mostly maintaining, trying to build specific things, or just showing up to feel good? Do you track metrics or go more by how you feel?
Do you have any regular meditation, journaling, or mindfulness practices? Or any spiritual/philosophical frameworks you lean on for perspective?

When things feel off — whether it's work stress, relationship friction, or just general burnout — what's your default way of processing it? Do you talk it through with people, work through it solo, seek professional help, or something else?
